concerns.text: Task T016 (Validation logic) and T025 (Consistency check) are placed AFTER the implementation tasks (T011-T013) but T025 is marked as a 'Validation Gate' that must run BEFORE bulk benchmarking (T021). However, T025 depends on T016, which depends on T011-T013. The ordering is correct for implementation, but T025 is listed in Phase 3 (US1) while T021 is in Phase 4 (US2). This is acceptable, BUT T025 description says 'requires T016, T011-T013' which is correct. The issue is T016 is listed as [P] (parallel) but it logically requires T011-T013 to be complete. T016 cannot be parallel with T011-T013. Marking T016 as [P] is a semantic violation of the parallel-safe constraint.

Task T021 (BenchmarkRunner) depends on T022a (Generate dataset subsets) and T035 (Theoretical baseline). T022a depends on T006. T035 is marked [P] but its description says 'MUST run before T021 and T023'. This creates a hidden ordering dependency: T035 must complete before T021 starts, yet T035 is marked [P] implying it can run in parallel with T021. This is a semantic violation. T035 should not be [P] if it is a strict prerequisite for T021.

Task T061 (Project-level aggregation) depends on T043 (CV enforcement). T043 depends on T042. T042 is marked [P]. T043 is NOT marked [P]. T061 is marked [P]. However, T061 reads the output of T043 ('results/benchmarks/cv_summary.csv'). If T043 is not complete, T061 cannot run. Marking T061 as [P] implies it can run in parallel with T043, which is impossible. This is a semantic violation of the parallel-safe constraint.

Task T006 (Synthetic data generator) and T007 (Directory structure) are in Phase 2. T022a (Generate dataset subsets) in Phase 4 depends on T006. This is correct. However, T007 creates 'tools/pre-commit-checksum.py' and installs it as a git hook. This is a setup task that should ideally be in Phase 1 (Setup) or clearly separated from the data generation logic. While not a hard ordering violation, it mixes infrastructure setup with data logic.

Task T029a (Data grouping) and T029b (Data serialization) are split. T029b depends on T029a. T028 (Kruskal-Wallis) depends on T029a and T029b. T029a and T029b are marked [P]. T029b cannot be parallel with T029a. Marking T029b as [P] is a semantic violation.
